speaker: CADE
text: Fie on ambitions! Fie on myself, that have a

speaker: CADE
text: sword and yet am ready to famish! These five days

speaker: CADE
text: have I hid me in these woods and durst not peep

speaker: CADE
text: out, for all the country is laid for me. But now am

speaker: CADE
text: I so hungry that, if I might have a lease of my life

speaker: CADE
text: for a thousand years, I could stay no longer.

speaker: CADE
text: Wherefore, o’er a brick wall have I climbed into

speaker: CADE
text: this garden, to see if I can eat grass, or pick a sallet

speaker: CADE
text: another while, which is not amiss to cool a man’s

speaker: CADE
text: stomach this hot weather. And I think this word

speaker: CADE
text: sallet was born to do me good; for many a time,

speaker: CADE
text: but for a sallet, my brainpan had been cleft with a

speaker: CADE
text: brown bill; and many a time, when I have been dry

speaker: CADE
text: and bravely marching, it hath served me instead of

speaker: CADE
text: a quart pot to drink in; and now the word sallet

speaker: CADE
text: must serve me to feed on.

speaker: IDEN
text: Lord, who would live turmoilèd in the court

speaker: IDEN
text: And may enjoy such quiet walks as these?

speaker: IDEN
text: This small inheritance my father left me

speaker: IDEN
text: Contenteth me, and worth a monarchy.

speaker: IDEN
text: I seek not to wax great by others’ waning,

speaker: IDEN
text: Or gather wealth, I care not with what envy.

speaker: IDEN
text: Sufficeth that I have maintains my state

speaker: IDEN
text: And sends the poor well pleasèd from my gate.

speaker: CADE
text: Here’s the lord of the soil come to seize

speaker: CADE
text: me for a stray, for entering his fee-simple without

speaker: CADE
text: leave.—Ah, villain, thou wilt betray me and get a

speaker: CADE
text: thousand crowns of the King by carrying my head

speaker: CADE
text: to him; but I’ll make thee eat iron like an ostrich

speaker: CADE
text: and swallow my sword like a great pin, ere thou

speaker: CADE
text: and I part.

speaker: IDEN
text: Why, rude companion, whatsoe’er thou be,

speaker: IDEN
text: I know thee not. Why, then, should I betray thee?

speaker: IDEN
text: Is ’t not enough to break into my garden

speaker: IDEN
text: And, like a thief, to come to rob my grounds,

speaker: IDEN
text: Climbing my walls in spite of me the owner,

speaker: IDEN
text: But thou wilt brave me with these saucy terms?

speaker: CADE
text: Brave thee? Ay, by the best blood that ever was

speaker: CADE
text: broached, and beard thee too. Look on me well: I

speaker: CADE
text: have eat no meat these five days, yet come thou

speaker: CADE
text: and thy five men, and if I do not leave you all as

speaker: CADE
text: dead as a doornail, I pray God I may never eat

speaker: CADE
text: grass more.

speaker: IDEN
text: Nay, it shall ne’er be said, while England stands,

speaker: IDEN
text: That Alexander Iden, an esquire of Kent,

speaker: IDEN
text: Took odds to combat a poor famished man.

speaker: IDEN
text: Oppose thy steadfast gazing eyes to mine;

speaker: IDEN
text: See if thou canst outface me with thy looks.

speaker: IDEN
text: Set limb to limb, and thou art far the lesser;

speaker: IDEN
text: Thy hand is but a finger to my fist,

speaker: IDEN
text: Thy leg a stick comparèd with this truncheon.

speaker: IDEN
text: My foot shall fight with all the strength thou hast;

speaker: IDEN
text: And if mine arm be heavèd in the air,

speaker: IDEN
text: Thy grave is digged already in the earth.

speaker: IDEN
text: As for words, whose greatness answers words,

speaker: IDEN
text: Let this my sword report what speech forbears.

speaker: CADE
text: By my valor, the most complete champion that

speaker: CADE
text: ever I heard! Steel, if thou turn the edge or cut not

speaker: CADE
text: out the burly-boned clown in chines of beef ere

speaker: CADE
text: thou sleep in thy sheath, I beseech God on my

speaker: CADE
text: knees thou mayst be turned to hobnails.

speaker: CADE
text: O, I am slain! Famine, and no other, hath slain me.

speaker: CADE
text: Let ten thousand devils come against me, and give

speaker: CADE
text: me but the ten meals I have lost, and I’d defy them

speaker: CADE
text: all. Wither, garden, and be henceforth a burying

speaker: CADE
text: place to all that do dwell in this house, because the

speaker: CADE
text: unconquered soul of Cade is fled.

speaker: IDEN
text: Is ’t Cade that I have slain, that monstrous traitor?

speaker: IDEN
text: Sword, I will hallow thee for this thy deed,

speaker: IDEN
text: And hang thee o’er my tomb when I am dead.

speaker: IDEN
text: Ne’er shall this blood be wipèd from thy point,

speaker: IDEN
text: But thou shalt wear it as a herald’s coat

speaker: IDEN
text: To emblaze the honor that thy master got.

speaker: CADE
text: Iden, farewell, and be proud of thy victory. Tell

speaker: CADE
text: Kent from me she hath lost her best man, and

speaker: CADE
text: exhort all the world to be cowards; for I, that never

speaker: CADE
text: feared any, am vanquished by famine, not by valor.

speaker: IDEN
text: How much thou wrong’st me, heaven be my judge!

speaker: IDEN
text: Die, damnèd wretch, the curse of her that bare thee!

speaker: IDEN
text: And as I thrust thy body in with my sword,

speaker: IDEN
text: So wish I, I might thrust thy soul to hell.

speaker: IDEN
text: Hence will I drag thee headlong by the heels

speaker: IDEN
text: Unto a dunghill, which shall be thy grave,

speaker: IDEN
text: And there cut off thy most ungracious head,

speaker: IDEN
text: Which I will bear in triumph to the King,

speaker: IDEN
text: Leaving thy trunk for crows to feed upon.
